prompt: Design a hybrid methodology for ProjectNova, a regulatory fintech platform with agile development teams and waterfall compliance requirements. 12-month timeline, 4 dev squads, SOX and PCI-DSS audit obligations.
assertions: assigns agile approach to development workstreams with clear rationale
assigns predictive approach to compliance/regulatory workstreams
defines integration seams between agile dev and predictive compliance
includes sprint-to-milestone mapping for unified timeline
addresses SOX and PCI-DSS audit trail requirements explicitly
provides a hybrid topology map or decision matrix
does not force a single methodology across all workstreams
evidence tags present on key decisions (not solely [SUPUESTO])

prompt: Our agile teams track velocity in story points and our PMO requires Earned Value Management reports with SPI and CPI. Map sprint velocity metrics to waterfall EVM for integrated reporting across both methodologies.
assertions: defines Planned Value formula using story points and cost-per-point
defines Earned Value formula using accepted story points
explains SPI and CPI calculation from agile-derived EV/PV/AC
addresses the sprint cadence to reporting period alignment
warns about assumptions in story-point-to-cost conversion with [SUPUESTO] tags
provides a unified dashboard schema or table showing both agile and EVM metrics
does not recommend abandoning either velocity tracking or EVM

prompt: Our team does 2-week sprints but all requirements were gathered upfront in a 3-month analysis phase, testing happens only after the last sprint, and the product owner cannot re-prioritize the backlog. Assess our process for hybrid anti-patterns.
assertions: identifies water-scrum-fall as primary anti-pattern with specific evidence
detects agile-in-name-only (AINO) due to PO lacking prioritization authority
flags sequential testing phase as contradicting agile principles
classifies severity for each finding (Critical, High, Medium, Low)
provides specific remediation actions, not generic advice
includes a health score or severity assessment for the overall implementation
references vertical slicing or incremental delivery as remediation

prompt: We are 4 months into a 12-month pure waterfall project and need to transition to hybrid mid-execution. Requirements phase is complete, design is 80% done, and construction has not started. How do we transition without losing existing work?
assertions: preserves completed requirements and design artifacts as baseline
recommends introducing agile for construction phase forward
defines how existing waterfall artifacts translate to agile backlog items
addresses team readiness and potential need for agile coaching
creates a transition timeline with specific milestones
maintains governance continuity — existing stage gates remain valid
warns about risks of mid-project methodology change with evidence tags
does not recommend discarding completed waterfall deliverables
